Toddalia asiatica, also known as "Asian Toddalia" or "Toddavaddia", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, known for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antidiabetic agent. The bioactive constituents of Toddalia asiatica include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Toddalia asiatica include decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
